issue.body:
It appears that the `LINT4002` bslint rule incorrectly scans for the component name _anywhere_ in the `CreateObject` call. Instead, it should only look for them directly following a "roSGNode". 

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<component name="Parent" extends="Group">
</component>
```

```brightscript
? createObject("Parent") 'bslint should NOT consider this valid usage of the `Parent` component
? createObject("roSGNode", "Parent") ' THIS is the proper usage of `Parent` component 
```
issue.title: Incorrect usage of CreateObject for component usage

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Clears the outstanding high-severity audit advisory and aligns the audit gate with the other RokuCommunity repos.

- Bump `form-data` `2.5.5` → `2.5.6` (lockfile), clearing the CRLF advisory GHSA-hmw2-7cc7-3qxx. It comes in transitively via `brighterscript` → `roku-deploy` → `@types/request` (which allows `^2.5.5`), so no manifest change is needed. Same bump the sibling repos already merged.
- Raise the `npm audit` step to `--audit-level=high` so the audit script only gates on high and above.

No high/critical advisories remain; `build`, `lint`, and tests pass.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Security enhancements

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## Summary

- Adds GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq (`uuid` <11.1.1, missing buffer bounds check in `v3()`/`v5()`/`v6()`) to the audit-ci allowlist.
- Sets up `audit-ci` config and wires the `audit` script through it.
- Adds an `Accepted security advisories` section to the README.

## Why it's safe to defer

The vulnerable code path is `v3()`/`v5()`/`v6()` when a caller-provided output buffer is passed. Every consumer in this dep graph (`postman-request`, dev-only `nyc>istanbul-lib-processinfo`) calls only `v4()` with no buffer arg, so the vulnerable path is unreachable. See `audit-ci.jsonc` for the exact reasoning.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Accept GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq (uuid <11.1.1) advisory

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Adds a required gate job to the security audit workflow so audit failures can be enforced selectively — blocking on scheduled runs and release branch PRs, but informational-only on feature branch PRs.

## Changes

- **`.github/workflows/security-audit.yml`**: Added `security-audit-required` job that:
  - Runs `always()` after the `audit` job
  - Exits non-zero (enforces failure) only when triggered by non-PR events or PRs from `release/` branches
  - Sets `permissions: {}` to follow least-privilege principle

```yaml
  security-audit-required:
    needs: audit
    if: always()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions: {}
    steps:
      - if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' || startsWith(github.head_ref, 'release/')
        run: if [ "${{ needs.audit.result }}" = "failure" ]; then exit 1; fi
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

>     if: always()
>     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
>     steps:
>       - if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' || startsWith(github.head_ref, 'release/')
>         run: if [ "${{ needs.audit.result }}" = "failure" ]; then exit 1; fi
> ```

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
This PR syncs v1 with the latest changes from master and upgrades bslint to brighterscript@1.0.0-alpha.51.

It includes CI and maintenance updates such as moving workflows to Node 18.20.4, switching coverage reporting to the Coveralls GitHub Action, adding a scheduled security audit workflow, refreshing package metadata/lockfile, updating README badges, and carrying forward changelog entries. It also includes small test fixes needed for compatibility with the updated toolchain and BrighterScript release
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Update to latest from master and Brighterscript-1.0.0-alpha.51

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## Summary
- Resolves all 15 `npm audit` advisories (1 low, 5 moderate, 7 high, 2 critical) → **0 vulnerabilities**.
- Adds a dedicated nightly security audit workflow plus visible status badge.
- Modernizes CI tooling (Node 18.20.4, mocha 10.x) and replaces the deprecated `coveralls-next` package with the `coverallsapp/github-action@v2` step.

## Changes
**Dependencies (`package.json`)**
- Drop `coveralls-next` dev dep and `publish-coverage` script
- Bump `mocha` → `^10.8.2`, `@types/mocha` → `^10.0.10`
- Add top-level `overrides` with `serialize-javascript: ^7.0.5`
- Add `"audit": "npm audit"` script
- Add `"lcovonly"` to `nyc.reporter`

**CI workflows**
- `build.yml`: Node `14.18.1` → `18.20.4`; coveralls reporting restored via `coverallsapp/github-action@v2` (gated to `ubuntu-latest`); `COVERALLS_REPO_TOKEN` moved from job env into the action's env
- `create-package.yml`: Node `14.19.0` → `18.20.4`
- `make-release-artifacts.yml`: Node `14.18.1` → `18.20.4`
- New `security-audit.yml`: runs `npm run audit` on push to master, every PR, and nightly at 06:00 UTC

**README**
- New `security` status badge (with inline padlock SVG `logo=`, since shields.io otherwise falls back to "build" for this endpoint)
- `&label=npm` added to existing `npm/dm/*` and `npm/v/*` badges so they read "npm" instead of "downloads"/"npm version"
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Security enhancements

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## Summary
- New `for-terminator-style` rule (`'end-for' | 'next' | 'off'`, default `'end-for'`) flags inconsistent terminators on `for` / `for each` loops.
- Code actions in both directions (`Convert to end for` / `Convert to next`) — replace just the terminator token, preserving indentation and any trailing comment.
- `WhileStatement` is unaffected (the parser does not accept `next` for `while`); nested loops are flagged and fixed independently.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Add for-terminator-style rule

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
This PR creates the `v0.8.42` release of `bslint`. Here are some useful links:
- [GitHub Release](https://github.com/rokucommunity/bslint/releases/tag/v0.8.42)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/rokucommunity/bslint/blob/v0.8.42/CHANGELOG.md)
- [See what's changed](https://github.com/rokucommunity/bslint/compare/v0.8.42...v0.8.42)

## Summary

- Previously, `TrackCodeFlow`, `CodeStyle`, and `CheckUsage` were each registered as separate `CompilerPlugin` instances, causing each BRS file to be walked multiple times per validation pass
- Now a single `bslint-inner` plugin is registered per program (via closure in `afterProgramCreate`), and for BRS files **TrackCodeFlow drives one combined walk** with CodeStyle's visitor injected inline via a new `extraVisitor` parameter — eliminating the redundant second walk
- `CodeStyle.afterFileValidate` is retained for backward compatibility (direct/standalone usage in tests and external tooling)

## Changes

**`src/plugins/codeStyle/index.ts`**
- `createBrsVisitor(diags)` — returns a walk-compatible visitor without walking, for injection into TrackCodeFlow's walk
- `collectBrsPreWalkDiagnostics(file, diags)` — eol-last + regex checks (no AST walk needed)
- `checkTopLevelComments(file, diags)` — handles comments outside function bodies (missed by the per-function walk)
- `collectBrsFunctionDiagnostics(file, diags)` — function keyword/type annotation style checks
- `afterFileValidate` retained using a full `file.ast.walk` for standalone use

**`src/plugins/trackCodeFlow/index.ts`**
- `afterFileValidate` accepts optional `extraVisitor` called for every node in the existing per-function walk at zero extra walk overhead

**`src/index.ts`**
- Three separate `program.plugins.add(...)` calls replaced with a single `bslint-inner` plugin per program
- All lifecycle hooks (`afterFileValidate`, `afterScopeValidate`, `onGetCodeActions`) dispatched from one plugin via closure — no WeakMap lookups needed in hot paths

## Test plan
- [x] All 107 existing tests pass (`npm test`)
- [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly (`npx tsc --noEmit`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
The `unused-parameter` rule wasn't flagging unused parameters in functions without local variables:

```brightscript
sub error2(hey as string)
    print "nope"  ' 'hey' was not being flagged as unused
end sub
```

The `finalize()` function only ran when a `locals` map existed. Functions without local variables never created this map, so parameter checking was skipped.

This PR modifies `closeBlock()` to always call `finalize()` when closing the function body, passing an empty map if needed.

Fixes #173
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Fix parameters not getting flagged as unused

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### BSLINT Ignore Matching Fix - Summary

#### Problem
`ignores` patterns in `bslint.json` were loaded correctly but not applied reliably, especially for entries like:
- `**/roku_modules/**/*`
- `source/promises.*`

This caused ignored files to still produce bslint diagnostics (e.g. `BSLINT3004`, `BSLINT3018`).

#### Root Cause
`createContext().ignores()` matched globs only against `file.pathAbsolute` (absolute filesystem path).
Your glob patterns are typically repo-relative. Combined with path-format differences (absolute vs relative, slash normalization), `minimatch` often failed to match.

This issue was reproduced in a Linux environment, but the underlying mismatch is platform-sensitive (Linux absolute paths and Windows separator differences), so the fix normalizes both patterns and candidate paths.

#### Fix Applied
Updated ignore matching logic in util to:
1. Normalize both patterns and paths to forward slashes (`/`)
2. Strip leading slashes for consistent repo-style matching
3. Match each pattern against multiple candidate paths:
   - `file.pkgPath` (preferred relative path)
   - `file.srcPath`
   - `file.pathAbsolute` (fallback)

So ignores now work across Linux/Windows path styles and relative/absolute path inputs.

#### Validation Result
After patching and rerunning `npm run build:lib`:
- `source/roku_modules/...` files were correctly ignored
- `source/promises.d.bs` was correctly ignored
- bslint no longer reported style errors for those ignored files
- build proceeded successfully (only non-bslint warning remained)

#### Recommendation
Use this as the upstream fix in `src/util.ts` (not a config workaround in `bslint.json`), since the issue is matcher behavior, not user config.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Fix ignores matching by normalizing file paths and matching against pkgPath/srcPath

This PR creates the `v0.8.36` release of `bslint`. Here are some useful links:

This enables bslint to flag unused function parameters. The roku compiler already flags these, so it would be helpful if bslint could catch them as well.

~~I considered adding a separate rule, but I didn't think it was worth the added complexity. Popular linters like ESLint flag unused local variables and function parameters as a single rule (https://eslint.org/docs/latest/rules/no-unused-vars), so I figured that bslint could follow suit.~~

I've added a new rule, `unused-parameter` to allow users to enforce this check.

Fixes #72

## Summary

feat: add json schema for bslint.json and generate types from it

## Details

- creates a `bslint.schema.json` given the current supported properties and types supported by bslint
- ~uses `json2ts` to auto generate the types from the schema to prevent duplicate maintenance.~
- ~add a npm prebuild step to ensure types are generated before TSC compilation~

solves https://github.com/rokucommunity/bslint/issues/104

## How it was tested

- reference the new schema in a local setup using
```
  "$schema": "./node_modules/@rokucommunity/bslint/bslint.schema.json",
 ```
- running `npm run build` and `npm run test` in this project

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
The `all-except-single-line` option will enforce "always" for multiple line AA but for single line AA it will enforce "no-dangling".

The `all-except-single-line` option is here to help with creating more line independent when an AA is extended.   For multiple line AA, always is preferred. `Always` will  allow for the addition to AA at the end without requiring a change to the previous line. 

However for a single line AA, `always` just adds an extra `,` and does not create line independence. For a single line AA it is better to use `no-dangling`. This will keep the AA smaller.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Add new aa-comma-style all-except-single-line.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Given a function with `throw` statements in some branches, there is a false positive that considers those branches as invalid. A `throw` can be a valid exit strategy for a function when "something truly terrible happens". 

```
function ok7() as integer
    a = 1
    if a > 0
        throw "something wrong"
    else
        return 0
    end if
end function
```
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Avoid false positive for missing return
Add documentation for bslint usage with VSCode

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Roku recently introduced the paradigm of using underscores to indicate that a variable is unused. bslint should probably follow the same concept. 

Question: Should we revise the ` 'unused-variable'?: RuleSeverity;` to contain options instead of just severity? Perhaps something like:

```
{
    "unused-variable": "all" | "all-except-leading-underscores" | etc..
}
```
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Don't mark vars with leading underscores as unused

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
This was an issue when a function was used within the namespace it was declared in.

eg:

```bs
namespace SomeNamespace

  sub alpha() 
  end sub
  
  sub beta()
     alpha() ' This was previously giving LINT1001 error (Using uninitialised variable)
  end sub

end namespace
```

pull_requests_reviewed.title: Bump lodash from 4.17.20 to 4.17.21

pull_requests_reviewed.body: Similar to the `no-console` rules for eslint/tslint. Enforces the use of a logging framework rather than raw print statements.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: add no-print rule to enforce use of logger

pull_requests_reviewed.body: Fixes #16
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Prevent false positive by relaxing variable use

pull_requests_reviewed.body: Fixes #10
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Implement 'unused variable' detection

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Fixes #9 

```brightscript
sub ok8()
    a = Rnd(10)
    if a > 5
        b = a
    end if
    if b <> invalid ' narrow variable as safe
        print "b"; b
    end if
end sub

sub ok9()
    a = Rnd(10)
    if a > 5
        b = a
    end if
    if b = invalid ' narrowed
        print "b"; b
    end if
end sub

sub ok10()
    a = Rnd(10)
    if a > 5
        b = a
    end if
    if b = invalid ' narrowed exit
        return
    end if
    print "b"; b
end sub
```
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Narrow variables assignments when testing for `invalid`
